recent_activities_14d.subject: [Clari - Email Sent] Re: Invitation to participate in Nubank's SRM RFI — Zycus
[Clari - Email Received] Re: Invitation to participate in Nubank's SRM RFI — Zycus

our_open_deliverables.text: Send Horizon event marketing presentations and customer session materials to Ligia for review
Share case studies with KPIs (savings, cycle time) relevant to financial services or high-volume procurement with Eduardo and Ligia
Send Bogota event details and invitation to Eduardo for his specialist to attend
Quantify speed-to-value and TCO advantage of Zycus SaaS vs internal build (time-to-deploy, total cost of ownership, ongoing maintenance cost, Merlin AI R&D depth)
Provide LATAM customer references (Brazil or Mexico-headquartered) with published KPIs and benchmark data

buyer_dependent_items.text: Confirm Eduardo's approval threshold and whether CFO co-signs contracts above a certain ACV, and provide standard vendor contract template and MSA redline SLA
Shortlist 2-3 vendors from RFI responses and notify Zycus of advancement to RFP round

competitive_position.summary: Three-way competition: SAP Ariba (incumbent, replacing due to poor UX/customization, status DECLINING), Coupa (peer RFI participant, active threat), and build-in-house (parallel evaluation for some modules, faded for iSupplier/iSource). Ariba is most recent but weakest current threat (buyer confirmed replacement underway 18 Jun 2026). Coupa is live peer in RFI/shortlist, medium threat (Gartner peer, but no specific Nubank mention of Coupa strengths). Build-in-house is highest strategic threat for iContract/iRisk/Merlin modules (Eduardo: 'thinking more about build internally' 18 Jun) but confirmed buy-from-market for iSupplier/iSource/eRequest. Single strongest CURRENT threat: build-in-house for half the modules, followed by Coupa as RFI peer. Wedge: prove Zycus SaaS delivers end-to-end orchestration faster (3-6 months) and cheaper (TCO: SaaS OpEx + continuous R&D vs internal build ongoing dev/maintenance) than internal build, with better UX than Ariba and deeper AI (Merlin core, autonomous negotiation agent) than Coupa.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: We are using Ariba... quite honestly we receive a lot of complaints. Ariba is difficult tool to customize... culture of new bank is super agile, entrepreneur, fast mindset, avoid bureaucracy... when we have to implement S2P old school, difficult, super allergic, organization will not adapt to a tool
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus single-codebase S2P with built-in Merlin intake orchestration and user-friendly UX as seamless, low-bureaucracy alternative to Ariba's clunky, hard-to-customize platform. Prove Zycus can deliver agile configuration and fast deployment (3-6 months) vs Ariba's rigid, slow customization cycles. Emphasize 50%+ R&D investment (continuous innovation) vs SAP's slower enterprise cadence.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: I realized hey I need to implement here maybe a Coupa or an Ariba... your main competitors like Coupa, Jagger, Ariba, this is the market you are playing right? [Karson: Yes, Gartner Magic Quadrant top-right with Coupa and SAP]
competitors.source: Eduardo Freitas and Karson Keogh, 18 Jun 2026 call
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate Zycus vs Coupa on: (1) single organic codebase (Zycus) vs acquisition-based platform (Coupa bought multiple vendors, less seamless); (2) Merlin AI as core vs Coupa AI bolt-ons (Zycus autonomous negotiation agent, Merlin intake orchestration built-in, not acquired); (3) nimble privately-held (Zycus) vs public-company shareholder constraints (Coupa); (4) transparent modular SaaS pricing with staged rollout to prove speed-to-value and lower TCO. Provide side-by-side TCO model (Zycus SaaS OpEx + R&D vs Coupa licensing + ongoing maintenance) and LATAM case studies to validate regional support vs Coupa.

competitors.name: Build in-house
competitors.quote: This whole mindset in Silicon Valley software disruption... easier to create your own software... myself as learner thinking more about build internally our process or solutions... BUT we decided not to develop eSourcing and eRequest in-house, opting to acquire market solutions while balancing price, features, and integration
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify speed-to-value and TCO advantage of Zycus SaaS vs internal build: Zycus 3-6 months deploy + continuous R&D (50%+ workforce, Merlin AI depth, years of procurement domain expertise) vs 12-18 months internal build + ongoing dev/maintenance + distraction from core fintech product. Highlight pre-built SAP/Jira/Slack ReadyConnectors (vs custom-code integration cost/risk for internal build), Merlin AI orchestration (hard to replicate internally), and autonomous negotiation agent (years of R&D, market-tested). Offer sandbox/POC to prove Zycus can deliver tailored Jira → iSupplier → iSource → iContract → SAP orchestration faster and cheaper than internal build. Position AppExtend co-innovation as middle path: SaaS speed + custom flexibility without build cost/risk. Target modules at risk (iContract, iRisk, Merlin intake) and prove Zycus is better build partner than internal team.

champion_strength.source: Ligia last appeared 26 Sep 2025 demo call; Eduardo Freitas first call 18 Jun 2026; 6+ month gap
champion_strength.summary: Champion status is weak and at risk. Ligia Alvarenga was early advocate (Aug-Sep 2025) but has not appeared since Eduardo hire (6+ month gap); current role and advocacy unknown. No coach, internal advocate, or multi-threaded champion exists. Deal is single-threaded through Eduardo (new EB, 3 months tenure, first Zycus call 18 Jun 2026).
champion_strength.champion: Ligia Alvarenga (former Head of Procurement, current role unknown)

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Eduardo Freitas, Procurement Director, eduardo.freitas@nubank.com.br
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Eduardo Freitas, Procurement Director, is the identified Economic Buyer. He joined Nubank 3 months prior to 18 Jun 2026 call (~Mar 2026) and reports directly to Nubank CFO. Eduardo stated 'I joined 3 months ago as procurement director... new bank decided to have a proper director for procurement' and 'my boss, the CFO of New Bank, shared me an invitation for your event' (18 Jun 2026). Eduardo controls vendor selection and RFI process: 'RFI that we ask you is the only one we already defined to go to the market... we are going to engage in the next phase with one of the vendors' (18 Jun). Eduardo drove 18 Jun discovery call, asked detailed pricing/business-model questions ('what is the business model behind any of those modules? Saas by volume, fixed fee?'), and explored build-vs-buy tradeoffs. CFO (Eduardo's manager, name unknown) forwarded Horizon event invite to Eduardo, signaling awareness, but CFO has not appeared on calls and current sentiment/sponsorship are unknown. Eduardo's approval threshold and whether CFO co-signs contracts above a certain ACV are unconfirmed. Risk: deal is single-threaded through Eduardo (new, 3 months tenure); if Eduardo's sign-off ceiling is below $100k (six-module deal), CFO must co-sign, and we have no CFO relationship. Next move: validate Eduardo's approval authority ('What is your sign-off threshold for SaaS contracts? Does CFO co-sign above a certain ACV?') and request intro to CFO to create second exec thread and confirm executive sponsorship.

recommended_moves: Provide modular staged-rollout pricing with clear per-module metrics and ROI breakeven timeline
Provide 2-3 LATAM case studies and offer reference call to validate regional support
Prepare hyper-tailored RFP demo mapped 1:1 to Nubank's Jira → Ariba → Netflix → SAP workflow
